duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Isolate liquidation transfers NFT to caller (params.msgSender) instead of the highest bidder (loanData.lastBidder), enabling anyone to steal auctioned NFTs using the bidder's escrowed funds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical code defect in executeIsolateLiquidate where NFT is sent to params.msgSender rather than loanData.lastBidder. Same file (IsolateLogic.sol), same function, same root cause.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: flashLoanERC20 intentionally omits nonReentrant modifier and does not update availableLiquidity during the loan, enabling reentrancy during callback to manipulate interest rates and protocol state using stale inflated liquidity
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same combined defect: missing nonReentrant on flashLoanERC20 plus availableLiquidity not decremented by erc20TransferOutOnFlashLoan. ECON-H010 (recursive nesting) is a specific amplification of the same root cause. All target FlashLoan.sol/FlashLoanLogic.sol with the same mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: executeIsolateRedeem decreases isolate borrow balance of params.msgSender instead of the actual borrower (tokenData.owner), corrupting per-user debt accounting and enabling third-party debt reduction
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All twelve threats describe the same code defect in IsolateLogic.executeIsolateRedeem where erc20DecreaseIsolateScaledBorrow is called with params.msgSender instead of tokenData.owner. OPS-H012 describes downstream consequences of the same bug. TECH2-H004 here refers to the first instance titled 'Isolate Redeem Decreases Caller's Debt'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Native token wrap/unwrap helpers use transferFrom incorrectly: wrapNativeTokenInWallet uses transferFrom(self, user) instead of transfer, and unwrapNativeTokenInWallet requires user WETH approval, breaking all native ETH deposit/repay/borrow/liquidation flows
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same root defect in VaultLogic.wrapNativeTokenInWallet and/or unwrapNativeTokenInWallet using transferFrom incorrectly, causing reverts across multiple native ETH entry points. TECH2-H003 here refers to the first instance titled 'All Native ETH Payable Functions Revert'. Different threats emphasize different entry points (deposit, borrow, repay, liquidate) but all stem from the same helper functions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BVault.withdrawERC20 passes original uncapped amount to unwrapNativeTokenInWallet after executeWithdrawERC20 may have internally capped it to userBalance, causing native ETH full-withdrawal patterns to revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same code defect where the local 'amount' variable in BVault.withdrawERC20 is not updated after executeWithdrawERC20 caps params.amount internally, causing the unwrap to use the wrong amount. TECH2-H005 here refers to the first instance titled 'Native ETH Withdrawals Revert When Requested Amount Exceeds Balance'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: executeFlashLoanERC721 checks tokenData.owner but does not check tokenData.lockerAddr, allowing flash-loaning of NFTs locked by yield managers or isolate lending, bypassing lock guarantees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All ten threats describe the same missing check in FlashLoanLogic.executeFlashLoanERC721 where tokenData.lockerAddr is not validated to be address(0). TECH2-H006 here refers to the first instance titled 'Locked NFTs Can Be Flash Loaned'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Flash loan ERC20 transfers bypass availableLiquidity validation, allowing flash loans to access bid escrow funds (totalBidAmout) and temporarily violating solvency invariants
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same issue: erc20TransferOutOnFlashLoan does not validate amount against availableLiquidity, enabling flash loans to temporarily access bid escrow funds.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: executeCollectFeeToTreasury reads and zeros accruedFee before calling updateInterestIndexs, causing order-of-operations errors in fee collection timing and share calculation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same order-of-operations defect in PoolLogic.executeCollectFeeToTreasury where accruedFee is zeroed before index update. TECH-H008 focuses on under-collection, OPS-H007 on inflated shares, ECON-H008 on both — all same root cause.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Flash loan ERC20 transfer functions (erc20TransferOutOnFlashLoan/erc20TransferInOnFlashLoan) lack pre/post balance delta verification, enabling value drain with fee-on-transfer tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing balance delta check in VaultLogic's flash loan transfer functions compared to the regular transfer functions that do have such checks.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross liquidation ERC721 per-item debt calculation uses integer division truncation (collateralTotalDebtToCover / userCollateralBalance) causing systematic underpayment by liquidators
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same integer division truncation in LiquidationLogic._calculateDebtAmountFromERC721Collateral, with the same code location and impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: rayMul/rayDiv rounding asymmetry causes amountScaled to exceed original userScaledCrossBorrow/userScaledCrossSupply by 1 wei during full debt repayment or liquidation, causing underflow reverts and potentially unliquidatable positions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the same fundamental rounding issue where rayMul followed by rayDiv can produce a value 1 greater than the original, causing underflow in borrow/supply subtraction operations. ECON-H016 targets the supply side (erc20TransferCrossSupply) while others target the borrow side, but the root mathematical defect and fix pattern are identical.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Isolate auction first bid must be >= borrowAmount, making underwater NFT positions (debt > market value) permanently unliquidatable, creating systemic bad debt
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same economic design flaw where the minimum bid requirement (>= borrowAmount) combined with calculateNftLoanLiquidatePrice flooring at borrowAmount prevents rational liquidation of underwater positions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Isolate auction push-based bid refund can be blocked by blocklisted/reverting previous bidder address, permanently freezing the auction
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same push-based refund anti-pattern in executeIsolateAuction where erc20TransferOutBidAmount to the previous bidder can revert if the bidder is blocklisted (e.g., USDC blocklist).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Isolate liquidation fails to clear tokenData.lockerAddr, causing liquidated NFTs to become permanently locked if re-deposited or if liquidator receives them with supplyAsCollateral=true
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same missing lockerAddr reset in VaultLogic's isolate liquidation functions (erc721DecreaseIsolateSupplyOnLiquidate / erc721TransferIsolateSupplyOnLiquidate), causing permanent NFT lock.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross liquidation ERC721 remainDebtToLiquidate is credited as borrower supply via erc20IncreaseCrossSupply after updateInterestRates, which does not account for this additional supply in rate calculations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same issue in LiquidationLogic.executeCrossLiquidateERC721 where remainDebtToLiquidate becomes supply without being reflected in the interest rate update.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Strict balance-delta invariant checks in VaultLogic transfer functions reject non-standard ERC20 tokens (fee-on-transfer, rebasing) despite documented support, causing operational DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same strict require(poolSizeAfter == poolSizeBefore ± amount) checks in VaultLogic's transfer functions that reject tokens with non-standard balance behavior.
